   153→      console.error('Failed to spawn PTY:', e)
   154→      const errorMsg = e?.message || String(e)
   155→      alert(`Failed to start Claude session:\n\n${errorMsg}\n\nPlease ensure Claude Code is installed and try restarting the application.`)
   156→    }
   157→  }, [api, addTab, openTabs, projects, setActiveTab, settings?.backend, setTileLayout])
   158→
   159→  const handleOpenSessionAtPosition = useCallback(async (projectPath: string, dropZone: DropZone | null, containerSize: { width: number; height: number }, currentLayout?: TileLayout[]) => {
   160→    // Use the effective layout passed from the drop handler (matches what computeDropZone used),
   161→    // falling back to the latest stored layout via ref (not the stale closure value)
   162→    const layoutToUse = currentLayout || tileLayoutRef.current
   163→    console.log('[App] handleOpenSessionAtPosition called with:', { projectPath, dropZone, containerSize, currentTileLayout: layoutToUse, hadCurrentLayout: !!currentLayout })
   164→
   165→    if (!projectPath || projectPath === 'pending') {
   166→      console.error('[App] Invalid project path:', projectPath)
   167→      return
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
   171→    console.log('[App] Found project:', project)
   172→    const effectiveBackend = (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
   173→      ? project.backend
   174→      : (settings?.backend && settings.backend !== 'default'
   175→        ? settings.backend
   176→        : 'claude')) as BackendId
   177→    console.log('[App] Using backend:', effectiveBackend)
   178→
   179→    // Always create a new session when dragging to a position
   180→    const sessionId: string | undefined = undefined
   181→    const slug: string | undefined = undefined
   182→
   183→    const projectName = projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || projectPath
   184→    const title = slug ? `${projectName} - ${slug}` : `${projectName} - New`
   185→
   186→    try {
   187→      await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(projectPath)
   188→      const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(projectPath, sessionId, undefined, effectiveBackend)
   189→
   190→      // Calculate the new layout based on the drop zone
   191→      let newLayout: TileLayout[]
   192→
   193→      // Re-read the latest layout after the async spawn (layout may have changed during await)
   194→      const latestLayout = currentLayout || tileLayoutRef.current
   195→
   196→      // Sidebar drag onto swap zone → always tab into target tile
   197→      if (dropZone && dropZone.type === 'swap') {
   198→        const targetTile = latestLayout.find(t => t.id === dropZone.targetTileId)
   199→        if (targetTile) {
   200→          console.log('[App] Adding as sub-tab to existing tile')
   201→          newLayout = addTabToExistingTile(latestLayout, targetTile.id, ptyId)
   202→        } else {
   203→          console.log('[App] Using addTileToLayout for swap (target not found)')
   204→          newLayout = addTileToLayout(latestLayout, ptyId, dropZone.targetTileId, containerSize.width, containerSize.height)
   205→        }
   206→      } else if (dropZone && dropZone.type !== 'swap') {
   207→        const direction = dropZone.type.replace('split-', '') as 'top' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'right'
   208→        console.log('[App] Using splitTile with direction:', direction, 'targetTileId:', dropZone.targetTileId)
   209→        newLayout = splitTile(latestLayout, dropZone.targetTileId, ptyId, direction)
   210→      } else {
   211→        console.log('[App] Using addTileToLayout with no drop zone (null)')
   212→        newLayout = addTileToLayout(latestLayout, ptyId, null, containerSize.width, containerSize.height)
   213→      }
   214→      console.log('[App] New layout calculated:', newLayout)
   215→
   216→      // Add tab FIRST, then set layout LAST.
   217→      // This ensures our positioned layout is the final state update and
   218→      // won't be overwritten by useEffectiveLayout's generic positioning
   219→      // if there's an intermediate render between these two updates.
   220→      addTab({
   221→        id: ptyId,
   222→        projectPath,
   223→        sessionId,
   224→        title,
   225→        ptyId,
   226→        backend: effectiveBackend
   227→      })
   228→      setTileLayout(newLayout)
   229→    } catch (e: any) {
   230→      console.error('Failed to spawn PTY:', e)
   231→      const errorMsg = e?.message || String(e)
   232→      alert(`Failed to start Claude session:\n\n${errorMsg}\n\nPlease ensure Claude Code is installed and try restarting the application.`)
   233→    }
   234→  }, [api, addTab, projects, openTabs, settings?.backend, setTileLayout])
   235→
   236→  const handleCloseTab = useCallback((tabId: string) => {
   237→    const tab = openTabs.find(t => t.id === tabId)
   238→    if (tab) {
   239→      closedTabsRef.current.push({
   240→        projectPath: tab.projectPath,
   241→        sessionId: tab.sessionId,
   242→        title: tab.title,
   243→        backend: tab.backend
   244→      })
   245→      setClosedTabCount(closedTabsRef.current.length)
   246→    }
   247→    api.killPty(tabId)
   248→    clearTerminalBuffer(tabId)
   249→    removeTab(tabId)
   250→  }, [api, openTabs, removeTab])
   251→
   252→  const handleCloseProjectTabs = useCallback((projectPath: string) => {
   253→    const tabsToClose = openTabs.filter(tab => tab.projectPath === projectPath)
   254→    tabsToClose.forEach(tab => {
   255→      closedTabsRef.current.push({
   256→        projectPath: tab.projectPath,
   257→        sessionId: tab.sessionId,
   258→        title: tab.title,
   259→        backend: tab.backend
   260→      })
   261→      api.killPty(tab.id)
   262→      clearTerminalBuffer(tab.id)
   263→      removeTab(tab.id)
   264→    })
   265→    setClosedTabCount(closedTabsRef.current.length)
   266→  }, [api, openTabs, removeTab])
   267→
   268→  const handleProjectCreated = useCallback((projectPath: string, projectName: string) => {
   269→    addProject({ path: projectPath, name: projectName })
   270→    handleOpenSession(projectPath, undefined, undefined, undefined, false)
   271→  }, [addProject, handleOpenSession])
   272→
   273→  const handleUndoCloseTab = useCallback(() => {
   274→    const info = closedTabsRef.current.pop()
   275→    if (!info) return
   276→    setClosedTabCount(closedTabsRef.current.length)
   277→    handleOpenSession(info.projectPath, info.sessionId)
   278→  }, [handleOpenSession])
   279→
   280→  return {
   281→    handleAddProject,
   282→    handleAddProjectsFromParent,
   283→    handleOpenSession,
   284→    handleOpenSessionAtPosition,
   285→    handleCloseTab,
   286→    handleCloseProjectTabs,
   287→    handleProjectCreated,
   288→    handleUndoCloseTab,
   289→    canUndoCloseTab: closedTabCount > 0
   290→  }
   291→}

   208→        console.log('[App] Using splitTile with direction:', direction, 'targetTileId:', dropZone.targetTileId)
   209→        newLayout = splitTile(latestLayout, dropZone.targetTileId, ptyId, direction)
   210→      } else {
   211→        console.log('[App] Using addTileToLayout with no drop zone (null)')
   212→        newLayout = addTileToLayout(latestLayout, ptyId, null, containerSize.width, containerSize.height)
   213→      }
   214→      console.log('[App] New layout calculated:', newLayout)
   215→
   216→      // Add tab FIRST, then set layout LAST.
   217→      // This ensures our positioned layout is the final state update and
   218→      // won't be overwritten by useEffectiveLayout's generic positioning
   219→      // if there's an intermediate render between these two updates.
   220→      addTab({
   221→        id: ptyId,
   222→        projectPath,
   223→        sessionId,
   224→        title,
   225→        ptyId,
   226→        backend: effectiveBackend
   227→      })
   228→      setTileLayout(newLayout)
   229→    } catch (e: any) {
   230→      console.error('Failed to spawn PTY:', e)
   231→      const errorMsg = e?.message || String(e)
   232→      alert(`Failed to start Claude session:\n\n${errorMsg}\n\nPlease ensure Claude Code is installed and try restarting the application.`)
   233→    }
   234→  }, [api, addTab, projects, openTabs, settings?.backend, setTileLayout])
   235→
   236→  const handleCloseTab = useCallback((tabId: string) => {
   237→    const tab = openTabs.find(t => t.id === tabId)
   238→    if (tab) {
   239→      closedTabsRef.current.push({
   240→        projectPath: tab.projectPath,
   241→        sessionId: tab.sessionId,
   242→        title: tab.title,
   243→        backend: tab.backend
   244→      })
   245→      setClosedTabCount(closedTabsRef.current.length)
   246→    }
   247→    api.killPty(tabId)
   248→    clearTerminalBuffer(tabId)
   249→    removeTab(tabId)
   250→  }, [api, openTabs, removeTab])
   251→
   252→  const handleCloseProjectTabs = useCallback((projectPath: string) => {
   253→    const tabsToClose = openTabs.filter(tab => tab.projectPath === projectPath)
   254→    tabsToClose.forEach(tab => {
   255→      closedTabsRef.current.push({
   256→        projectPath: tab.projectPath,
   257→        sessionId: tab.sessionId,
   258→        title: tab.title,
   259→        backend: tab.backend
   260→      })
   261→      api.killPty(tab.id)
   262→      clearTerminalBuffer(tab.id)
   263→      removeTab(tab.id)
   264→    })
   265→    setClosedTabCount(closedTabsRef.current.length)
   266→  }, [api, openTabs, removeTab])
   267→
   268→  const handleProjectCreated = useCallback((projectPath: string, projectName: string) => {
   269→    addProject({ path: projectPath, name: projectName })
   270→    handleOpenSession(projectPath, undefined, undefined, undefined, false)
   271→  }, [addProject, handleOpenSession])
   272→
   273→  const handleUndoCloseTab = useCallback(() => {
   274→    const info = closedTabsRef.current.pop()
   275→    if (!info) return
   276→    setClosedTabCount(closedTabsRef.current.length)
   277→    handleOpenSession(info.projectPath, info.sessionId)
   278→  }, [handleOpenSession])
   279→
   280→  return {
   281→    handleAddProject,
   282→    handleAddProjectsFromParent,
   283→    handleOpenSession,
   284→    handleOpenSessionAtPosition,
   285→    handleCloseTab,
   286→    handleCloseProjectTabs,
   287→    handleProjectCreated,
   288→    handleUndoCloseTab,
   289→    canUndoCloseTab: closedTabCount > 0
   290→  }
   291→}
   292→

    35→}: SidebarProps): React.ReactElement | null {
    36→  // Mobile detection
    37→  const { isMobile } = useIsMobile()
    38→
    39→  // Initialize state

    58→  })

    75→    onCloseProjectTabs,
    76→  })
    77→

    79→  const projectItemDeps = useProjectItemDependencies(state, callbacks, handlers, openTabs)
    80→

    98→        onOpenSession={(sessionId, slug, isNewSession) =>

   100→        }

1→import { useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
     2→import { Project, useWorkspaceStore } from '../../stores/workspace.js'
     3→import { SidebarState } from './useSidebarState.js'
     4→import { SidebarProps } from './types.js'
     5→
     6→// Use type assertion for extended electronAPI methods not in the base type
     7→const electronAPI = window.electronAPI as (typeof window.electronAPI) & {
     8→  selectExecutable?: () => Promise<string | null>
     9→  runExecutable?: (executable: string, cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
    10→  getCategoryMetaPath?: (categoryName: string) => Promise<string>
    11→  getMetaProjectsPath?: () => Promise<string>
    12→}
    13→
    14→export interface SidebarHandlers {
    15→  // Project handlers
    16→  handleSelectExecutable: (project: Project) => Promise<void>
    17→  handleClearExecutable: (project: Project) => void
    18→  handleRunExecutable: (project: Project) => Promise<void>
    19→
    20→  // Rename handlers
    21→  handleStartRename: (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => void
    22→  handleRenameSubmit: () => void
    23→  handleRenameKeyDown: (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => void
    24→
    25→  // Category handlers
    26→  handleAddCategory: () => void
    27→  handleOpenCategoryAsProject: (categoryName: string) => Promise<void>
    28→  handleOpenAllProjects: () => Promise<void>
    29→  handleStartCategoryRename: (
    30→    category: ReturnType<typeof useWorkspaceStore.getState>['categories'][0]
    31→  ) => void
    32→  handleCategoryRenameSubmit: () => void
    33→  handleCategoryRenameKeyDown: (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => void
    34→  toggleCategoryCollapse: (categoryId: string) => void
    35→
    36→  // Resize handler
    37→  handleMouseDown: (e: React.MouseEvent) => void
    38→
    39→  // Backdrop handler
    40→  handleBackdropClick: () => void
    41→}
    42→
    43→export interface UseSidebarHandlersParams {
    44→  state: SidebarState
    45→  onUpdateProject: SidebarProps['onUpdateProject']
    46→  onOpenSession: SidebarProps['onOpenSession']
    47→  isMobile: boolean
    48→  onMobileClose: (() => void) | undefined
    49→}
    50→
    51→export function useSidebarHandlers(params: UseSidebarHandlersParams): SidebarHandlers {
    52→  const { state, onUpdateProject, onOpenSession, isMobile, onMobileClose } = params
    53→  const {
    54→    setContextMenu,
    55→    editingProject,
    56→    setEditingProject,
    57→    editInputRef,
    58→    editingCategory,
    59→    setEditingCategory,
    60→    categoryEditInputRef,
    61→    addCategory,
    62→    updateCategory,
    63→    categories,
    64→    setIsResizing,
    65→    moveProjectToCategory,
    66→  } = state
    67→
    68→  // Project handlers
    69→  const handleSelectExecutable = useCallback(
    70→    async (project: Project) => {
    71→      const executable = await electronAPI?.selectExecutable?.()
    72→      if (executable) onUpdateProject(project.path, { executable })
    73→      setContextMenu(null)
    74→    },
    75→    [onUpdateProject, setContextMenu]
    76→  )
    77→
    78→  const handleClearExecutable = useCallback(
    79→    (project: Project) => {
    80→      onUpdateProject(project.path, { executable: undefined })
    81→      setContextMenu(null)
    82→    },
    83→    [onUpdateProject, setContextMenu]
    84→  )
    85→
    86→  const handleRunExecutable = useCallback(
    87→    async (project: Project) => {
    88→      if (project.executable) {
    89→        const result = await electronAPI?.runExecutable?.(project.executable, project.path)
    90→        if (result && !result.success) console.error('Failed to run executable:', result.error)
    91→      }
    92→      setContextMenu(null)
    93→    },
    94→    [setContextMenu]
    95→  )
    96→
    97→  // Rename handlers
    98→  // Ref tracks cancel intent so blur handler can skip saving after Escape
    99→  const renameCancelledRef = useRef(false)
   100→
   101→  const handleStartRename = useCallback(
   102→    (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => {
   103→      e.stopPropagation()
   104→      renameCancelledRef.current = false
   105→      setEditingProject({ path: project.path, name: project.name })
   106→      setTimeout(() => editInputRef.current?.select(), 0)
   107→    },
   108→    [setEditingProject, editInputRef]
   109→  )
   110→
   111→  const handleRenameSubmit = useCallback(() => {
   112→    if (renameCancelledRef.current) {
   113→      renameCancelledRef.current = false
   114→      return
   115→    }
   116→    if (editingProject && editingProject.name.trim()) {
   117→      onUpdateProject(editingProject.path, { name: editingProject.name.trim() })
   118→    }
   119→    setEditingProject(null)
   120→  }, [editingProject, onUpdateProject, setEditingProject])
   121→
   122→  const handleRenameKeyDown = useCallback(
   123→    (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
   124→      if (e.key === 'Enter') handleRenameSubmit()
   125→      else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   126→        renameCancelledRef.current = true
   127→        setEditingProject(null)
   128→      }
   129→    },

     1→import { useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
     2→import { Project, useWorkspaceStore } from '../../stores/workspace.js'
     3→import { SidebarState } from './useSidebarState.js'
     4→import { SidebarProps } from './types.js'
     5→
     6→// Use type assertion for extended electronAPI methods not in the base type
     7→const electronAPI = window.electronAPI as (typeof window.electronAPI) & {
     8→  selectExecutable?: () => Promise<string | null>
     9→  runExecutable?: (executable: string, cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
    10→  getCategoryMetaPath?: (categoryName: string) => Promise<string>
    11→  getMetaProjectsPath?: () => Promise<string>
    12→}
    13→
    14→export interface SidebarHandlers {
    15→  // Project handlers
    16→  handleSelectExecutable: (project: Project) => Promise<void>
    17→  handleClearExecutable: (project: Project) => void
    18→  handleRunExecutable: (project: Project) => Promise<void>
    19→
    20→  // Rename handlers
    21→  handleStartRename: (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => void
    22→  handleRenameSubmit: () => void
    23→  handleRenameKeyDown: (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => void
    24→
    25→  // Category handlers
    26→  handleAddCategory: () => void
    27→  handleOpenCategoryAsProject: (categoryName: string) => Promise<void>
    28→  handleOpenAllProjects: () => Promise<void>
    29→  handleStartCategoryRename: (
    30→    category: ReturnType<typeof useWorkspaceStore.getState>['categories'][0]
    31→  ) => void
    32→  handleCategoryRenameSubmit: () => void
    33→  handleCategoryRenameKeyDown: (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => void
    34→  toggleCategoryCollapse: (categoryId: string) => void
    35→
    36→  // Resize handler
    37→  handleMouseDown: (e: React.MouseEvent) => void
    38→
    39→  // Backdrop handler
    40→  handleBackdropClick: () => void
    41→}
    42→
    43→export interface UseSidebarHandlersParams {
    44→  state: SidebarState
    45→  onUpdateProject: SidebarProps['onUpdateProject']
    46→  onOpenSession: SidebarProps['onOpenSession']
    47→  isMobile: boolean
    48→  onMobileClose: (() => void) | undefined
    49→}
    50→
    51→export function useSidebarHandlers(params: UseSidebarHandlersParams): SidebarHandlers {
    52→  const { state, onUpdateProject, onOpenSession, isMobile, onMobileClose } = params
    53→  const {
    54→    setContextMenu,
    55→    editingProject,
    56→    setEditingProject,
    57→    editInputRef,
    58→    editingCategory,
    59→    setEditingCategory,
    60→    categoryEditInputRef,
    61→    addCategory,
    62→    updateCategory,
    63→    categories,
    64→    setIsResizing,
    65→    moveProjectToCategory,
    66→  } = state
    67→
    68→  // Project handlers
    69→  const handleSelectExecutable = useCallback(
    70→    async (project: Project) => {
    71→      const executable = await electronAPI?.selectExecutable?.()
    72→      if (executable) onUpdateProject(project.path, { executable })
    73→      setContextMenu(null)
    74→    },
    75→    [onUpdateProject, setContextMenu]
    76→  )
    77→
    78→  const handleClearExecutable = useCallback(
    79→    (project: Project) => {
    80→      onUpdateProject(project.path, { executable: undefined })
    81→      setContextMenu(null)
    82→    },
    83→    [onUpdateProject, setContextMenu]
    84→  )
    85→
    86→  const handleRunExecutable = useCallback(
    87→    async (project: Project) => {
    88→      if (project.executable) {
    89→        const result = await electronAPI?.runExecutable?.(project.executable, project.path)
    90→        if (result && !result.success) console.error('Failed to run executable:', result.error)
    91→      }
    92→      setContextMenu(null)
    93→    },
    94→    [setContextMenu]
    95→  )
    96→
    97→  // Rename handlers
    98→  // Ref tracks cancel intent so blur handler can skip saving after Escape
    99→  const renameCancelledRef = useRef(false)
   100→
   101→  const handleStartRename = useCallback(
   102→    (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => {
   103→      e.stopPropagation()
   104→      renameCancelledRef.current = false
   105→      setEditingProject({ path: project.path, name: project.name })
   106→      setTimeout(() => editInputRef.current?.select(), 0)
   107→    },
   108→    [setEditingProject, editInputRef]
   109→  )
   110→
   111→  const handleRenameSubmit = useCallback(() => {
   112→    if (renameCancelledRef.current) {
   113→      renameCancelledRef.current = false
   114→      return
   115→    }
   116→    if (editingProject && editingProject.name.trim()) {
   117→      onUpdateProject(editingProject.path, { name: editingProject.name.trim() })
   118→    }
   119→    setEditingProject(null)
   120→  }, [editingProject, onUpdateProject, setEditingProject])
   121→
   122→  const handleRenameKeyDown = useCallback(
   123→    (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
   124→      if (e.key === 'Enter') handleRenameSubmit()
   125→      else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   126→        renameCancelledRef.current = true
   127→        setEditingProject(null)
   128→      }
   129→    },
   130→    [handleRenameSubmit, setEditingProject]
   131→  )
   132→
   133→  // Category handlers

   135→    const newId = addCategory('New Category')

   137→    setTimeout(() => categoryEditInputRef.current?.select(), 0)
   138→  }, [addCategory, setEditingCategory, categoryEditInputRef])
   139→
   140→  const handleOpenCategoryAsProject = useCallback(
   141→    async (categoryName: string) => {
   142→      const metaPath = await electronAPI?.getCategoryMetaPath?.(categoryName)
   143→      if (metaPath) onOpenSession(metaPath)
   144→    },
   145→    [onOpenSession]
   146→  )
   147→
   148→  const handleOpenAllProjects = useCallback(async () => {
   149→    const metaPath = await electronAPI?.getMetaProjectsPath?.()
   150→    if (metaPath) onOpenSession(metaPath)

     2→import { Project } from '../../stores/workspace.js'
     3→import { SidebarState } from './useSidebarState.js'
     4→import { SidebarHandlers } from './useSidebarHandlers.js'
     5→import { OpenTab } from './types.js'
     6→
     7→export interface ProjectItemCallbacks {
     8→  handleProjectToggleExpand: (e: React.MouseEvent, projectPath: string) => void
     9→  handleProjectOpenSession: (
    10→    projectPath: string,
    11→    sessionId?: string,
    12→    slug?: string,
    13→    isNewSession?: boolean

    15→  handleProjectRunExecutable: (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>

    18→  handleProjectItemDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => void

    20→  handleProjectItemDrop: (
    21→    e: React.DragEvent,

    25→  handleProjectStartRename: (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => void
    26→  handleProjectEditingChange: (name: string) => void
    27→  renderProjectItem: (project: Project) => React.ReactElement
    28→}
    29→
    30→export interface UseProjectItemCallbacksParams {
    31→  state: SidebarState
    32→  handlers: SidebarHandlers
    33→  projects: Project[]
    34→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
    35→  onCloseProjectTabs: (projectPath: string) => void
    36→}
    37→
    38→export function useProjectItemCallbacks(
    39→  params: UseProjectItemCallbacksParams
    40→): Omit<ProjectItemCallbacks, 'renderProjectItem'> {
    41→  const { state, handlers, projects, onCloseProjectTabs } = params
    42→  const {
    43→    toggleProject,
    44→    handleOpenSession,
    45→    setContextMenu,
    46→    handleProjectDragStart,
    47→    handleProjectDragOver,
    48→    handleProjectDrop,
    49→    handleProjectDragEnd,
    50→    setEditingProject,
    51→  } = state
    52→  const { handleRunExecutable, handleStartRename } = handlers
    53→
    54→  const handleProjectToggleExpand = useCallback(
    55→    (e: React.MouseEvent, projectPath: string) => {
    56→      toggleProject(e, projectPath)
    57→    },
    58→    [toggleProject]
    59→  )
    60→
    61→  const handleProjectOpenSession = useCallback(
    62→    (projectPath: string, sessionId?: string, slug?: string, isNewSession?: boolean) => {
    63→      handleOpenSession(projectPath, sessionId, slug, isNewSession)
    64→    },
    65→    [handleOpenSession]
    66→  )
    67→
    68→  const handleProjectRunExecutable = useCallback(
    69→    async (projectPath: string) => {
    70→      const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
    71→      if (project) await handleRunExecutable(project)
    72→    },
    73→    [projects, handleRunExecutable]
    74→  )
    75→
    76→  const handleProjectCloseProjectTabs = useCallback(
    77→    (projectPath: string) => {
    78→      onCloseProjectTabs(projectPath)
    79→    },
    80→    [onCloseProjectTabs]
    81→  )
    82→
    83→  const handleProjectContextMenu = useCallback(
    84→    (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => {
    85→      e.preventDefault()
    86→      e.stopPropagation()
    87→      setContextMenu({ x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY, project })
    88→    },
    89→    [setContextMenu]
    90→  )
    91→
    92→  const handleProjectItemDragStart = useCallback(
    93→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => {
    94→      handleProjectDragStart(e, projectPath)
    95→    },
    96→    [handleProjectDragStart]
    97→  )
    98→
    99→  const handleProjectItemDragOver = useCallback(
   100→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => {
   101→      const rect = e.currentTarget.getBoundingClientRect()
   102→      const midY = rect.top + rect.height / 2
   103→      const position = e.clientY < midY ? 'before' : 'after'
   104→      handleProjectDragOver(e, projectPath, position)
   105→    },
   106→    [handleProjectDragOver]
   107→  )
   108→
   109→  const handleProjectItemDrop = useCallback(
   110→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string, categoryId: string | undefined) => {
   111→      handleProjectDrop(e, projectPath, categoryId)

   113→    [handleProjectDrop]
   114→  )

   116→  const handleProjectStartRename = useCallback(

   118→      handleStartRename(e, project)
   119→    },
   120→    [handleStartRename]
   121→  )
   122→
   123→  const handleProjectEditingChange = useCallback(
   124→    (name: string) => {
   125→      setEditingProject((prev) => (prev ? { ...prev, name } : null))
   126→    },
   127→    [setEditingProject]
   128→  )
   129→
   130→  return {
   131→    handleProjectToggleExpand,
   132→    handleProjectOpenSession,
   133→    handleProjectRunExecutable,
   134→    handleProjectCloseProjectTabs,
   135→    handleProjectContextMenu,
   136→    handleProjectItemDragStart,
   137→    handleProjectItemDragOver,
   138→    handleProjectItemDrop,
   139→    handleProjectStartRename,
   140→    handleProjectEditingChange,
   141→  }
   142→}
   143→
   144→// Separate hook to get the memoization dependencies for renderProjectItem
   145→export function useProjectItemDependencies(
   146→  state: SidebarState,
   147→  callbacks: Omit<ProjectItemCallbacks, 'renderProjectItem'>,
   148→  handlers: SidebarHandlers,
   149→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
   150→): unknown[] {
   151→  const {
   152→    expandedProject,
   153→    focusedProjectPath,
   154→    draggedProject,
   155→    editingProject,
   156→    sessions,
   157→    taskCounts,
   158→    dropTarget,
   159→    handleProjectDragEnd,
   160→  } = state
   161→  const { handleRenameSubmit, handleRenameKeyDown } = handlers
   162→
   163→  return [
   164→    expandedProject,
   165→    focusedProjectPath,
   166→    openTabs,
   167→    draggedProject,
   168→    editingProject,
   169→    sessions,
   170→    taskCounts,
   171→    dropTarget,

     1→import { useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { Project } from '../../stores/workspace.js'
     3→import { SidebarState } from './useSidebarState.js'
     4→import { SidebarHandlers } from './useSidebarHandlers.js'
     5→import { OpenTab } from './types.js'
     6→
     7→export interface ProjectItemCallbacks {
     8→  handleProjectToggleExpand: (e: React.MouseEvent, projectPath: string) => void
     9→  handleProjectOpenSession: (
    10→    projectPath: string,
    11→    sessionId?: string,
    12→    slug?: string,
    13→    isNewSession?: boolean
    14→  ) => void
    15→  handleProjectRunExecutable: (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>
    16→  handleProjectCloseProjectTabs: (projectPath: string) => void
    17→  handleProjectContextMenu: (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => void
    18→  handleProjectItemDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => void
    19→  handleProjectItemDragOver: (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => void
    20→  handleProjectItemDrop: (
    21→    e: React.DragEvent,
    22→    projectPath: string,
    23→    categoryId: string | undefined
    24→  ) => void
    25→  handleProjectStartRename: (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => void
    26→  handleProjectEditingChange: (name: string) => void
    27→  renderProjectItem: (project: Project) => React.ReactElement
    28→}
    29→
    30→export interface UseProjectItemCallbacksParams {
    31→  state: SidebarState
    32→  handlers: SidebarHandlers
    33→  projects: Project[]
    34→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
    35→  onCloseProjectTabs: (projectPath: string) => void
    36→}
    37→
    38→export function useProjectItemCallbacks(
    39→  params: UseProjectItemCallbacksParams
    40→): Omit<ProjectItemCallbacks, 'renderProjectItem'> {
    41→  const { state, handlers, projects, onCloseProjectTabs } = params
    42→  const {
    43→    toggleProject,
    44→    handleOpenSession,
    45→    setContextMenu,
    46→    handleProjectDragStart,
    47→    handleProjectDragOver,
    48→    handleProjectDrop,
    49→    handleProjectDragEnd,
    50→    setEditingProject,
    51→  } = state
    52→  const { handleRunExecutable, handleStartRename } = handlers
    53→
    54→  const handleProjectToggleExpand = useCallback(
    55→    (e: React.MouseEvent, projectPath: string) => {
    56→      toggleProject(e, projectPath)
    57→    },
    58→    [toggleProject]
    59→  )
    60→
    61→  const handleProjectOpenSession = useCallback(
    62→    (projectPath: string, sessionId?: string, slug?: string, isNewSession?: boolean) => {
    63→      handleOpenSession(projectPath, sessionId, slug, isNewSession)
    64→    },
    65→    [handleOpenSession]
    66→  )
    67→
    68→  const handleProjectRunExecutable = useCallback(
    69→    async (projectPath: string) => {
    70→      const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
    71→      if (project) await handleRunExecutable(project)
    72→    },
    73→    [projects, handleRunExecutable]
    74→  )
    75→
    76→  const handleProjectCloseProjectTabs = useCallback(
    77→    (projectPath: string) => {
    78→      onCloseProjectTabs(projectPath)
    79→    },
    80→    [onCloseProjectTabs]
    81→  )
    82→
    83→  const handleProjectContextMenu = useCallback(
    84→    (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => {
    85→      e.preventDefault()
    86→      e.stopPropagation()
    87→      setContextMenu({ x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY, project })
    88→    },
    89→    [setContextMenu]
    90→  )
    91→
    92→  const handleProjectItemDragStart = useCallback(
    93→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => {
    94→      handleProjectDragStart(e, projectPath)
    95→    },
    96→    [handleProjectDragStart]
    97→  )
    98→
    99→  const handleProjectItemDragOver = useCallback(
   100→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => {
   101→      const rect = e.currentTarget.getBoundingClientRect()
   102→      const midY = rect.top + rect.height / 2
   103→      const position = e.clientY < midY ? 'before' : 'after'
   104→      handleProjectDragOver(e, projectPath, position)
   105→    },
   106→    [handleProjectDragOver]
   107→  )
   108→
   109→  const handleProjectItemDrop = useCallback(
   110→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string, categoryId: string | undefined) => {
   111→      handleProjectDrop(e, projectPath, categoryId)
   112→    },
   113→    [handleProjectDrop]
   114→  )
   115→
   116→  const handleProjectStartRename = useCallback(
   117→    (e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => {
   118→      handleStartRename(e, project)
   119→    },
   120→    [handleStartRename]
   121→  )
   122→
   123→  const handleProjectEditingChange = useCallback(
   124→    (name: string) => {
   125→      setEditingProject((prev) => (prev ? { ...prev, name } : null))
   126→    },
   127→    [setEditingProject]
   128→  )
   129→
   130→  return {
   131→    handleProjectToggleExpand,
   132→    handleProjectOpenSession,
   133→    handleProjectRunExecutable,
   134→    handleProjectCloseProjectTabs,
   135→    handleProjectContextMenu,
   136→    handleProjectItemDragStart,
   137→    handleProjectItemDragOver,
   138→    handleProjectItemDrop,
   139→    handleProjectStartRename,
   140→    handleProjectEditingChange,
   141→  }
   142→}
   143→
   144→// Separate hook to get the memoization dependencies for renderProjectItem
   145→export function useProjectItemDependencies(
   146→  state: SidebarState,
   147→  callbacks: Omit<ProjectItemCallbacks, 'renderProjectItem'>,
   148→  handlers: SidebarHandlers,
   149→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
   150→): unknown[] {
   151→  const {
   152→    expandedProject,
   153→    focusedProjectPath,
   154→    draggedProject,
   155→    editingProject,
   156→    sessions,
   157→    taskCounts,
   158→    dropTarget,
   159→    handleProjectDragEnd,
   160→  } = state
   161→  const { handleRenameSubmit, handleRenameKeyDown } = handlers
   162→
   163→  return [
   164→    expandedProject,
   165→    focusedProjectPath,
   166→    openTabs,
   167→    draggedProject,
   168→    editingProject,
   169→    sessions,
   170→    taskCounts,
   171→    dropTarget,
   172→    callbacks.handleProjectToggleExpand,
   173→    callbacks.handleProjectOpenSession,
   174→    callbacks.handleProjectRunExecutable,
   175→    callbacks.handleProjectCloseProjectTabs,
   176→    callbacks.handleProjectContextMenu,
   177→    callbacks.handleProjectItemDragStart,
   178→    handleProjectDragEnd,
   179→    callbacks.handleProjectItemDragOver,
   180→    callbacks.handleProjectItemDrop,
   181→    callbacks.handleProjectStartRename,
   182→    callbacks.handleProjectEditingChange,
   183→    handleRenameSubmit,
   184→    handleRenameKeyDown,
   185→  ]
   186→}
   187→

import React, { useState, useRef, useMemo } from 'react'
import { Project, useWorkspaceStore } from '../../stores/workspace.js'
import { useVoice } from '../../contexts/VoiceContext.js'
import { SidebarProps, OpenTab, ClaudeSession } from './types.js'
import { useSessions, useDragAndDrop, useProjectSettingsModal } from './hooks/index.js'

export interface SidebarState {
  // Voice context
  volume: number
  setVolume: (v: number) => void
  speed: number
  setSpeed: (s: number) => void
  skipOnNew: boolean
  setSkipOnNew: (s: boolean) => void
  voiceOutputEnabled: boolean

  // Workspace store
  categories: ReturnType<typeof useWorkspaceStore.getState>['categories']
  addCategory: (name: string) => string
  updateCategory: (id: string, updates: Partial<{ name: string; collapsed: boolean }>) => void
  removeCategory: (id: string) => void
  reorderCategories: (categoryIds: string[]) => void
  moveProjectToCategory: (projectPath: string, categoryId: string | null) => void
  reorderProjects: (categoryId: string | null, projectPaths: string[]) => void

  // Custom hooks
  expandedProject: string | null
  sessions: Record<string, ClaudeSession[]>
  toggleProject: (e: React.MouseEvent, projectPath: string) => void
  handleOpenSession: (
    projectPath: string,
    sessionId?: string,
    slug?: string,
    isNewSession?: boolean
  ) => void

  // Drag and drop
  draggedProject: string | null
  draggedCategory: string | null
  dropTarget: { type: 'category' | 'project' | 'uncategorized'; id: string | null; position?: 'before' | 'after' } | null
  handleProjectDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => void
  handleProjectDragEnd: () => void
  handleCategoryDragOver: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => void
  handleCategoryDrop: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => void
  handleProjectDragOver: (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string, position: 'before' | 'after') => void
  handleProjectDrop: (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string, categoryId: string | undefined) => void
  handleCategoryDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string) => void
  handleCategoryDragEnd: () => void
  handleCategoryHeaderDragOver: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string, position: 'before' | 'after') => void
  handleCategoryHeaderDrop: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string) => void

  // Project settings modal
  projectSettingsModal: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['projectSettingsModal']
  installedVoices: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['installedVoices']
  globalVoiceSettings: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['globalVoiceSettings']
  globalPermissions: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['globalPermissions']
  apiStatus: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['apiStatus']
  setApiStatus: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['setApiStatus']
  handleOpenProjectSettings: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['handleOpenProjectSettings']
  handleSaveProjectSettings: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['handleSaveProjectSettings']
  handleToggleApi: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['handleToggleApi']
  setProjectSettingsModal: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['setProjectSettingsModal']
  handleToggleTool: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['handleToggleTool']
  handleAllowAll: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['handleAllowAll']
  handleClearAll: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['handleClearAll']
  handleProjectSettingsChange: ReturnType<typeof useProjectSettingsModal>['handleProjectSettingsChange']

  // Local state
  beadsExpanded: boolean
  setBeadsExpanded: (v: boolean) => void
  isResizing: boolean
  setIsResizing: (v: boolean) => void
  contextMenu: { x: number; y: number; project: Project } | null
  setContextMenu: (v: { x: number; y: number; project: Project } | null) => void
  categoryContextMenu: {
    x: number
    y: number
    category: ReturnType<typeof useWorkspaceStore.getState>['categories'][0]
  } | null
  setCategoryContextMenu: (
    v: {
      x: number
      y: number
      category: ReturnType<typeof useWorkspaceStore.getState>['categories'][0]
    } | null
  ) => void
  editingProject: { path: string; name: string } | null
  setEditingProject: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<{ path: string; name: string } | null>>
  editingCategory: { id: string; name: string } | null
  setEditingCategory: (v: { id: string; name: string } | null) => void
  isDebugMode: boolean
  setIsDebugMode: (v: boolean) => void
  extensionBrowserModal: { project: Project } | null
  setExtensionBrowserModal: (v: { project: Project } | null) => void
  claudeMdEditorModal: { project: Project } | null
  setClaudeMdEditorModal: (v: { project: Project } | null) => void
  deleteConfirmModal: { project: Project } | null
  setDeleteConfirmModal: (v: { project: Project } | null) => void
  taskCounts: Record<string, { open: number; inProgress: number }>
  setTaskCounts: (v: Record<string, { open: number; inProgress: number }>) => void

  // Refs
  sidebarRef: React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement>
  editInputRef: React.RefObject<HTMLInputElement>
  categoryEditInputRef: React.RefObject<HTMLInputElement>

  // Computed values
  focusedTabId: string | null
  focusedTab: OpenTab | undefined
  focusedProjectPath: string | null
  focusedTabPtyId: string | null
  beadsProjectPath: string | null
  focusedProject: Project | undefined
  sortedCategories: ReturnType<typeof useWorkspaceStore.getState>['categories']
  projectsByCategory: Record<string, Project[]>
}

export interface UseSidebarStateParams {
  projects: Project[]
  openTabs: OpenTab[]
  activeTabId: string | null
  lastFocusedTabId: string | null
  onOpenSession: SidebarProps['onOpenSession']
  onSwitchToTab: SidebarProps['onSwitchToTab']
  onUpdateProject: SidebarProps['onUpdateProject']
}

export function useSidebarState(params: UseSidebarStateParams): SidebarState {
  const { projects, openTabs, activeTabId, lastFocusedTabId, onOpenSession, onSwitchToTab, onUpdateProject } = params

  // Voice context
  const { volume, setVolume, speed, setSpeed, skipOnNew, setSkipOnNew, voiceOutputEnabled } =
    useVoice()

  // Workspace store
  const categories = useWorkspaceStore((state) => state.categories)
  const addCategory = useWorkspaceStore((state) => state.addCategory)
  const updateCategory = useWorkspaceStore((state) => state.updateCategory)
  const removeCategory = useWorkspaceStore((state) => state.removeCategory)
  const reorderCategories = useWorkspaceStore((state) => state.reorderCategories)
  const moveProjectToCategory = useWorkspaceStore((state) => state.moveProjectToCategory)
  const reorderProjects = useWorkspaceStore((state) => state.reorderProjects)

  // Custom hooks for extracted logic
  const { expandedProject, sessions, toggleProject, handleOpenSession } = useSessions({
    projects,
    openTabs,
    onOpenSession,
    onSwitchToTab,
  })

  const {
    draggedProject,
    draggedCategory,
    dropTarget,
    handleProjectDragStart,
    handleProjectDragEnd,
    handleCategoryDragOver,
    handleCategoryDrop,
    handleProjectDragOver,
    handleProjectDrop,
    handleCategoryDragStart,
    handleCategoryDragEnd,
    handleCategoryHeaderDragOver,
    handleCategoryHeaderDrop,
  } = useDragAndDrop({
    projects,
    moveProjectToCategory,
    reorderProjects,
    reorderCategories,
    categories,
  })

  const {
    projectSettingsModal,
    installedVoices,
    globalVoiceSettings,
    globalPermissions,
    apiStatus,
    setApiStatus,
    handleOpenProjectSettings,
    handleSaveProjectSettings,
    handleToggleApi,
    setProjectSettingsModal,
    handleToggleTool,
    handleAllowAll,
    handleClearAll,
    handleProjectSettingsChange,
  } = useProjectSettingsModal({ onUpdateProject })

  // Local state
  const [beadsExpanded, setBeadsExpanded] = useState(true)
  const [isResizing, setIsResizing] = useState(false)
  const [contextMenu, setContextMenu] = useState<{ x: number; y: number; project: Project } | null>(
    null
  )
  const [categoryContextMenu, setCategoryContextMenu] = useState<{
    x: number
    y: number
    category: ReturnType<typeof useWorkspaceStore.getState>['categories'][0]

     2→import { Project } from '../../../stores/workspace.js'

     4→
     5→interface UseDragAndDropOptions {
     6→  projects: Project[]
     7→  moveProjectToCategory: (projectPath: string, categoryId: string | null) => void
     8→  reorderProjects: (categoryId: string | null, projectPaths: string[]) => void
     9→  reorderCategories: (categoryIds: string[]) => void
    10→  categories: Array<{ id: string; name: string; order: number; collapsed?: boolean }>
    11→}
    12→
    13→interface UseDragAndDropReturn {
    14→  draggedProject: string | null
    15→  draggedCategory: string | null
    16→  dropTarget: DropTarget | null
    17→  handleProjectDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => void
    18→  handleProjectDragEnd: () => void
    19→  handleCategoryDragOver: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => void
    20→  handleCategoryDrop: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => void
    21→  handleProjectDragOver: (
    22→    e: React.DragEvent,
    23→    projectPath: string,
    24→    position: 'before' | 'after'
    25→  ) => void
    26→  handleProjectDrop: (
    27→    e: React.DragEvent,
    28→    targetPath: string,
    29→    targetCategoryId: string | undefined
    30→  ) => void
    31→  handleCategoryDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string) => void
    32→  handleCategoryDragEnd: () => void
    33→  handleCategoryHeaderDragOver: (
    34→    e: React.DragEvent,
    35→    targetCategoryId: string,
    36→    position: 'before' | 'after'
    37→  ) => void
    38→  handleCategoryHeaderDrop: (e: React.DragEvent, targetCategoryId: string) => void
    39→}
    40→
    41→export function useDragAndDrop({
    42→  projects,
    43→  moveProjectToCategory,
    44→  reorderProjects,
    45→  reorderCategories,
    46→  categories,
    47→}: UseDragAndDropOptions): UseDragAndDropReturn {
    48→  const [draggedProject, setDraggedProject] = useState<string | null>(null)
    49→  const [draggedCategory, setDraggedCategory] = useState<string | null>(null)
    50→  const [dropTarget, setDropTarget] = useState<DropTarget | null>(null)
    51→  // Ref tracks the latest dropTarget so drop handlers always read fresh position
    52→  // (state in the closure may be stale if React hasn't re-rendered since the last dragOver)
    53→  const dropTargetRef = useRef<DropTarget | null>(null)
    54→
    55→  const handleProjectDragStart = useCallback((e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string) => {
    56→    setDraggedProject(projectPath)
    57→    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move'
    58→    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', projectPath)
    59→    // Also set custom type so TiledTerminalView can detect sidebar project drags
    60→    e.dataTransfer.setData('application/x-sidebar-project', projectPath)
    61→  }, [])
    62→
    63→  const handleProjectDragEnd = useCallback(() => {
    64→    setDraggedProject(null)
    65→    setDropTarget(null)
    66→    dropTargetRef.current = null
    67→  }, [])
    68→
    69→  const handleCategoryDragOver = useCallback(
    70→    (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => {
    71→      e.preventDefault()
    72→      e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
    73→      if (draggedProject) {
    74→        const target: DropTarget = {
    75→          type: categoryId ? 'category' : 'uncategorized',
    76→          id: categoryId,
    77→        }
    78→        setDropTarget(target)
    79→        dropTargetRef.current = target
    80→      }
    81→    },
    82→    [draggedProject]
    83→  )
    84→
    85→  const handleCategoryDrop = useCallback(
    86→    (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => {
    87→      e.preventDefault()
    88→      if (draggedProject) {
    89→        moveProjectToCategory(draggedProject, categoryId)
    90→      }
    91→      setDraggedProject(null)
    92→      setDropTarget(null)
    93→      dropTargetRef.current = null
    94→    },
    95→    [draggedProject, moveProjectToCategory]
    96→  )
    97→
    98→  const handleProjectDragOver = useCallback(
    99→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string, position: 'before' | 'after') => {
   100→      e.preventDefault()
   101→      e.stopPropagation()
   102→      e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
   103→      if (draggedProject && draggedProject !== projectPath) {
   104→        const target: DropTarget = { type: 'project', id: projectPath, position }
   105→        setDropTarget(target)
   106→        dropTargetRef.current = target
   107→      }
   108→    },
   109→    [draggedProject]
   110→  )
   111→
   112→  const handleProjectDrop = useCallback(
   113→    (e: React.DragEvent, targetPath: string, targetCategoryId: string | undefined) => {
   114→      e.preventDefault()
   115→      e.stopPropagation()
   116→
   117→      if (draggedProject && draggedProject !== targetPath) {
   118→        const draggedProjectData = projects.find((p) => p.path === draggedProject)
   119→        const targetProject = projects.find((p) => p.path === targetPath)
   120→
   121→        if (draggedProjectData && targetProject) {
   122→          moveProjectToCategory(draggedProject, targetCategoryId ?? null)
   123→          const categoryProjects = projects
   124→            .filter((p) =>
   125→              targetCategoryId ? p.categoryId === targetCategoryId : !p.categoryId
   126→            )
   127→            .sort((a, b) => (a.order ?? 0) - (b.order ?? 0))
   128→            .map((p) => p.path)
   129→
   130→          const newOrder = categoryProjects.filter((p) => p !== draggedProject)
   131→          const targetIndex = newOrder.indexOf(targetPath)
   132→          // Read from ref to get the latest position (state may be stale)
   133→          const currentDropTarget = dropTargetRef.current
   134→          const insertIndex = currentDropTarget?.position === 'after' ? targetIndex + 1 : targetIndex
   135→          newOrder.splice(insertIndex, 0, draggedProject)
   136→          reorderProjects(targetCategoryId ?? null, newOrder)
   137→        }
   138→      }
   139→      setDraggedProject(null)
   140→      setDropTarget(null)
   141→      dropTargetRef.current = null
   142→    },
   143→    [draggedProject, projects, moveProjectToCategory, reorderProjects]
   144→  )
   145→
   146→  const handleCategoryDragStart = useCallback((e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string) => {
   147→    setDraggedCategory(categoryId)
   148→    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move'
   149→    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', categoryId)
   150→  }, [])
   151→
   152→  const handleCategoryDragEnd = useCallback(() => {
   153→    setDraggedCategory(null)
   154→    setDropTarget(null)
   155→    dropTargetRef.current = null
   156→  }, [])
   157→
   158→  const handleCategoryHeaderDragOver = useCallback(
   159→    (e: React.DragEvent, targetCategoryId: string, position: 'before' | 'after') => {
   160→      e.preventDefault()
   161→      e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
   162→      if (draggedCategory && draggedCategory !== targetCategoryId) {
   163→        const target: DropTarget = { type: 'category', id: targetCategoryId, position }
   164→        setDropTarget(target)
   165→        dropTargetRef.current = target
   166→      }
   167→    },
   168→    [draggedCategory]
   169→  )
   170→
   171→  const handleCategoryHeaderDrop = useCallback(
   172→    (e: React.DragEvent, targetCategoryId: string) => {
   173→      e.preventDefault()
   174→      if (draggedCategory && draggedCategory !== targetCategoryId) {
   175→        const orderedCategories = [...categories].sort((a, b) => a.order - b.order)
   176→        const categoryIds = orderedCategories.map((c) => c.id)
   177→        const newOrder = categoryIds.filter((id) => id !== draggedCategory)
   178→        const targetIndex = newOrder.indexOf(targetCategoryId)
   179→        // Read from ref to get the latest position (state may be stale)
   180→        const currentDropTarget = dropTargetRef.current
   181→        const insertIndex = currentDropTarget?.position === 'after' ? targetIndex + 1 : targetIndex
   182→        newOrder.splice(insertIndex, 0, draggedCategory)
   183→        reorderCategories(newOrder)
   184→      }
   185→      setDraggedCategory(null)
   186→      setDropTarget(null)
   187→      dropTargetRef.current = null
   188→    },
   189→    [draggedCategory, categories, reorderCategories]
   190→  )
   191→
   192→  return {
   193→    draggedProject,
   194→    draggedCategory,
   195→    dropTarget,
   196→    handleProjectDragStart,
   197→    handleProjectDragEnd,
   198→    handleCategoryDragOver,
   199→    handleCategoryDrop,
   200→    handleProjectDragOver,

2. The contents of these key files related to workspace persistence and settings:

content.text:
Now I have gathered sufficient information. Let me create a comprehensive bug audit report:

## BUG AUDIT REPORT: Workspace Persistence and Settings

Based on a thorough examination of the codebase, I've identified several potential bugs and architectural concerns related to workspace persistence and settings management in this Electron app.

---

### **1. CRITICAL: Race Condition in Workspace Save During Drag-and-Drop Reordering**

**Location:** `src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` lines 170-201

**Issue:** The workspace save effect depends on `[api, projects, openTabs, activeTabId, loading, viewMode, tileLayout, categories]`. When users drag and reorder projects in the sidebar, the following race condition can occur:

1. `reorderProjects()` is called (updates workspace store)
2. React re-renders with new project order
3. `useEffect` triggers save to disk
4. Meanwhile, `handleProjectDrop` calls both `moveProjectToCategory()` AND `reorderProjects()` in sequence

**Problem:** If the save completes before all state updates are flushed, the disk file could be written with a partial state. The `reorderProjects` and `moveProjectToCategory` are separate operations that might not atomically update the store.

**Evidence:**
```typescript
// src/renderer/components/sidebar/hooks/useDragAndDrop.ts:112-144
const handleProjectDrop = useCallback(
  (e: React.DragEvent, targetPath: string, targetCategoryId: string | undefined) => {
    // ...
    moveProjectToCategory(draggedProject, targetCategoryId ?? null)  // First update
    // ...
    reorderProjects(targetCategoryId ?? null, newOrder)  // Second update
```

The save effect triggers on ANY change to projects/categories. With two separate state updates, there's a window where the state is inconsistent.

**Risk:** Data loss of project ordering on app crash between updates.

---

### **2. HIGH: Incomplete Error Handling in SessionStore.save()**

**Location:** `src/main/session-store.ts` lines 166-201

**Issue:** The save method has multiple error handling gaps:

```typescript
private save(): void {
  try {
    const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
    JSON.parse(json)
    
    // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
    if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
      try {
        // ... backup logic with nested try-catch
      } catch (e) {
        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
        // !! CONTINUES EXECUTION - doesn't fail the save
      }
    }
    
    const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
    writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
    // !! NO ERROR HANDLING if writeFileSync fails
    
    const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
    JSON.parse(written)
    
    renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
    // !! Silently ignores ALL errors including disk full, permission denied
  }
}
```

**Problems:**
- If backup creation fails, save continues anyway
- If `writeFileSync` throws, there's no catch → outer catch silently swallows it
- If `renameSync` fails, temp file is orphaned
- No return value indicates success/failure to callers
- Callers have no way to know if save actually succeeded

**Risk:** Silent data loss when disk is full, permissions change, or filesystem errors occur.

---

### **3. HIGH: useViewState Hook Never Persists to Disk**

**Location:** `src/renderer/hooks/useViewState.ts`

**Issue:** The `useViewState` hook initializes UI state but:
- Returns state setters for `viewMode`, `tileLayout`, `sidebarWidth`, `sidebarCollapsed`
- These are never wired to disk persistence
- MainApp.tsx saves workspace BUT doesn't save view-only UI state like `sidebarWidth`

**Evidence:**
```typescript
// src/renderer/hooks/useViewState.ts - NO persistence
const [sidebarWidth, setSidebarWidth] = useState(280)
const [sidebarCollapsed, setSidebarCollapsed] = useState(false)

// src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx:185-199
api.saveWorkspace({
  // ... workspace data
  viewMode,
  tileLayout,
  categories
  // !! Missing: sidebarWidth, sidebarCollapsed
})
```

**Risk:** 
- Sidebar width resets to 280 on every app restart
- Sidebar collapse state is lost
- User experience regression between sessions

---

### **4. HIGH: Missing Serialization of Optional Tab Fields**

**Location:** `src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` lines 187-193

**Issue:** When saving tabs, several important fields are not being serialized:

```typescript
openTabs: openTabs.map(t => ({
  id: t.id,
  projectPath: t.projectPath,
  sessionId: t.sessionId,
  title: t.title,
  ptyId: t.ptyId,
  backend: t.backend
  // !! Missing: sessionId (already there, but let's check OpenTab interface)
}))
```

Looking at `OpenTab` interface in `src/main/session-store.ts`:
```typescript
export interface OpenTab {
  id: string
  projectPath: string
  sessionId?: string  // ✓ included
  title: string
  ptyId?: string      // ✓ included (as ptyId)
  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'  // ✓ included
}
```

**However**, in renderer's `OpenTab` (src/renderer/stores/workspace.ts):
```typescript
export interface OpenTab {
  id: string
  projectPath: string
  sessionId?: string
  title: string
  ptyId: string  // Required, not optional!
  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
}
```

**Risk:** Type mismatch could cause undefined ptyId issues during restore.

---

### **5. MEDIUM: Settings Persistence Split Across Multiple Calls**

**Location:** `src/renderer/components/SettingsModal.tsx` lines 158-180

**Issue:** Settings are saved via two separate async calls with no coordination:

```typescript
async function handleSave(): Promise<void> {
  const newSettings = {
    defaultProjectDir: general.defaultProjectDir,
    theme: general.selectedTheme,
    themeCustomization: general.themeCustomization,
    autoAcceptTools: general.autoAcceptTools,
    permissionMode: general.permissionMode,
    backend: general.backend
  }
  await window.electronAPI?.saveSettings(newSettings)  // Call 1
  // NO ERROR HANDLING
  await window.electronAPI?.voiceApplySettings?.({     // Call 2
    ttsVoice: voice.selectedVoice,
    ttsEngine: voice.selectedEngine,
    ttsSpeed: voice.ttsSpeed,
    xttsTemperature: xtts.temperature,
    xttsTopK: xtts.topK,
    xttsTopP: xtts.topP,
    xttsRepetitionPenalty: xtts.repetitionPenalty
  })
  onSaved?.(newSettings)  // !! Reports success before second call completes
  onClose()
}
```

**Problems:**
- If `voiceApplySettings` fails, `onSaved` callback already fired
- Settings are reported saved but voice settings might have failed
- No error boundaries or validation
- No user feedback if either call fails

**Risk:** Partial settings applied - theme saves but voice settings fail silently.

---

### **6. MEDIUM: Empty Workspace Protection Has Edge Case**

**Location:** `src/main/session-store.ts` lines 214-225

**Issue:** The protection against overwriting populated workspaces has a loophole:

```typescript
saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
  const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
  const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
  if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
    console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
    return  // !! Silently returns - no error thrown
  }
  this.data.workspace = workspace
  this.save()
  syncMetaProjects(workspace)
}
```

**Problems:**
- **Silent failure:** Callers don't know the save was rejected
- **Inconsistent state:** `this.data.workspace` was NOT updated, but caller thinks it was
- **No error callback:** No way to notify user or app of rejection
- Renderer component at line 185 calls `api.saveWorkspace()` but has no error handling

**Risk:**
- Frontend state and disk state diverge silently
- User makes changes, thinks they're saved, but they're not
- Next app restart shows old data, losing in-memory changes

---

### **7. MEDIUM: Race Condition in Session Restore and Tab Recreation**

**Location:** `src/renderer/hooks/useWorkspaceLoader.ts` lines 88-156

**Issue:** Complex async tab restoration with potential race conditions:

```typescript
if (workspace.openTabs && workspace.openTabs.length > 0) {
  const currentTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
  const usedSessionIds = new Set(currentTabs.map(t => t.sessionId).filter(Boolean))
  const sessionsCache = new Map<string, { list: { sessionId: string; slug: string }[]; nextIndex: number }>()

  for (const savedTab of workspace.openTabs) {
    try {
      // ... session discovery and spawning
      const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(...)  // Async call
      if (savedTab.id) {
        idMapping.set(savedTab.id, ptyId)  // Stores mapping
      }
      addTab({...})  // Updates store
      // !! Between await and addTab, another effect could run
    } catch (e) {
      console.error('Failed to restore tab:', ...)  // Silent catch
    }
  }
}
```

**Problems:**
- `for-await` loop with `addTab` calls can cause intermediate renders
- Each `await api.spawnPty()` is a checkpoint where layout can change
- `idMapping` is built during restore but used later - if restore order matters, this breaks
- No centralized transaction for multi-tab restore

**Risk:** Tabs restored out of order, layout mismatches, lost tabs if spawnPty fails mid-loop.

---

### **8. MEDIUM: Backup File Strategy Has No Expiry**

**Location:** `src/main/session-store.ts` lines 174-187

**Issue:** Backup is created but:
- Only one backup kept (no versioning)
- Backup can become stale if new saves fail
- No validation that backup is actually usable

```typescript
if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
  try {
    const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
    if (stat.size > 0) {
      // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
      const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
      if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
        writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))  // Overwrites old backup
      }
    }
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
  }
}
```

**Problems:**
- If a bad save happens and backup succeeds, next startup might load corrupted data
- Single backup means no rollback to earlier good state
- Backup file isn't validated during load

**Risk:** Corrupted workspace can persist across restarts if backup is bad.

---

### **9. MEDIUM: No Validation of Persisted Workspace Structure**

**Location:** `src/main/session-store.ts` lines 118-131

**Issue:** The load function checks minimal structure:

```typescript
private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
  try {
    if (!existsSync(path)) return null
    const stat = statSync(path)
    if (stat.size === 0) return null
    const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    const data = JSON.parse(content)
    if (!data?.workspace) return null  // !! Only checks workspace key exists
    return data
  } catch (e) {
    console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
    return null
  }
}
```

**Problems:**
- Doesn't validate `workspace.projects` is an array
- Doesn't validate `workspace.openTabs` is an array
- Doesn't validate project/tab object shapes
- Invalid data is returned and used as-is

**Risk:** Malformed workspace data could crash the app or cause undefined behavior downstream.

---

### **10. LOW: Project Rename Can Lose Changes During Escape**

**Location:** `src/renderer/components/sidebar/useSidebarHandlers.ts` lines 97-131

**Issue:** Rename cancel logic relies on a ref flag:

```typescript
const renameCancelledRef = useRef(false)

const handleStartRename = useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent, project: Project) => {
  e.stopPropagation()
  renameCancelledRef.current = false
  setEditingProject({ path: project.path, name: project.name })
  // ...
}, [])

const handleRenameKeyDown = useCallback((e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
  if (e.key === 'Enter') handleRenameSubmit()
  else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
    renameCancelledRef.current = true  // Set flag
    setEditingProject(null)  // Clear state
  }
}, [handleRenameSubmit, setEditingProject])
```

**Problem:** The `onBlur` handler runs before `onKeyDown` completes:
- User presses Escape
- `onKeyDown` sets `renameCancelledRef.current = true`
- Immediately, field loses focus
- `onBlur` calls `handleRenameSubmit()`
- `handleRenameSubmit` checks the ref - should be true

This is likely working correctly, but the pattern is fragile because the ref state must be read at the right time in the execution order.

---

### **11. LOW: Sidebar Width Not Persisted - Minor UX Issue**

**Location:** Multiple files

The sidebar width (`sidebarWidth`) and collapse state (`sidebarCollapsed`) from `useViewState` are never saved to disk. Every app restart resets the sidebar to default width and expanded state, even though the app saves `viewMode` and `tileLayout`.

---

### **SUMMARY OF CRITICAL FINDINGS:**

| Severity | Count | Issue |
|----------|-------|-------|
| **Critical** | 1 | Race condition in drag-reorder save |
| **High** | 3 | Incomplete error handling, missing UI persistence, settings split save |
| **Medium** | 6 | Backup strategy, validation gaps, session restore ordering |
| **Low** | 2 | Sidebar state loss, rename cancel fragility |

### **RECOMMENDATIONS:**

1. **Debounce workspace saves** to batch multiple store updates before writing disk
2. **Return error status** from `saveWorkspace()` and `saveSettings()` with proper error handling
3. **Validate persisted data** on load with TypeScript type guards
4. **Implement transaction-like semantics** for multi-step saves (settings + voice + workspace)
5. **Add telemetry/warnings** when saves are rejected or fail
6. **Persist sidebar UI state** along with other workspace data
7. **Keep rotating backups** (e.g., workspace.json, workspace.json.backup, workspace.json.backup2)
8. **Add smoke tests** for save/load roundtrips


Now I have gathered sufficient information. Let me create a comprehensive bug audit report:

## BUG AUDIT REPORT: Workspace Persistence and Settings

Based on a thorough examination of the codebase, I've identified several potential bugs and architectural concerns related to workspace persistence and settings management in this Electron app.

---

### **1. CRITICAL: Race Condition in Workspace Save During Drag-and-Drop Reordering**

**Location:** `src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` lines 170-201

**Issue:** The workspace save effect depends on `[api, projects, openTabs, activeTabId, loading, viewMode, tileLayout, categories]`. When users drag and reorder projects in the sidebar, the following race condition can occur:

1. `reorderProjects()` is called (updates workspace store)
2. React re-renders with new project order
3. `useEffect` triggers save to disk
4. Meanwhile, `handleProjectDrop` calls both `moveProjectToCategory()` AND `reorderProjects()` in sequence

**Problem:** If the save completes before all state updates are flushed, the disk file could be written with a partial state. The `reorderProjects` and `moveProjectToCategory` are separate operations that might not atomically update the store.

**Evidence:**
```typescript
// src/renderer/components/sidebar/hooks/useDragAndDrop.ts:112-144
const handleProjectDrop = useCallback(
  (e: React.DragEvent, targetPath: string, targetCategoryId: string | undefined) => {
    // ...
    moveProjectToCategory(draggedProject, targetCategoryId ?? null)  // First update
    // ...
    reorderProjects(targetCategoryId ?? null, newOrder)  // Second update
```

The save effect triggers on ANY change to projects/categories. With two separate state updates, there's a window where the state is inconsistent.

**Risk:** Data loss of project ordering on app crash between updates.

---

### **2. HIGH: Incomplete Error Handling in SessionStore.save()**

**Location:** `src/main/session-store.ts` lines 166-201

**Issue:** The save method has multiple error handling gaps:

```typescript
private save(): void {
  try {
    const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
    JSON.parse(json)
    
    // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
    if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
      try {
        // ... backup logic with nested try-catch
      } catch (e) {
        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
        // !! CONTINUES EXECUTION - doesn't fail the save
      }
    }
    
    const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
    writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
    // !! NO ERROR HANDLING if writeFileSync fails
    
    const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
    JSON.parse(written)
    
    renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
    // !! Silently ignores ALL errors including disk full, permission denied
  }
}
```

**Problems:**
- If backup creation fails, save continues anyway
- If `writeFileSync` throws, there's no catch → outer catch silently swallows it
- If `renameSync` fails, temp file is orphaned
- No return value indicates success/failure to callers
- Callers have no way to know if save actually succeeded

**Risk:** Silent data loss when disk is full, permissions change, or filesystem errors occur.

---

### **3. HIGH: useViewState Hook Never Persists to Disk**

**Location:** `src/renderer/hooks/useViewState.ts`

**Issue:** The `useViewState` hook initializes UI state but:
- Returns state setters for `viewMode`, `tileLayout`, `sidebarWidth`, `sidebarCollapsed`
- These are never wired to disk persistence
- MainApp.tsx saves workspace BUT doesn't save view-only UI state like `sidebarWidth`

**Evidence:**
```typescript
// src/renderer/hooks/useViewState.ts - NO persistence
const [sidebarWidth, setSidebarWidth] = useState(280)
const [sidebarCollapsed, setSidebarCollapsed] = useState(false)

// src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx:185-199
api.saveWorkspace({
  // ... workspace data
  viewMode,
  tileLayout,
  categories
  // !! Missing: sidebarWidth, sidebarCollapsed
})
```

**Risk:** 
- Sidebar width resets to 280 on every app restart
- Sidebar collapse state is lost
- User experience regression between sessions

---

### **4. HIGH: Missing Serialization of Optional Tab Fields**

**Location:** `src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` lines 187-193

**Issue:** When saving tabs, several important fields are not being serialized:

```typescript
openTabs: openTabs.map(t => ({
  id: t.id,
  projectPath: t.projectPath,
  sessionId: t.sessionId,
  title: t.title,
  ptyId: t.ptyId,
  backend: t.backend
  // !! Missing: sessionId (already there, but let's check OpenTab interface)
}))
```

Looking at `OpenTab` interface in `src/main/session-store.ts`:
```typescript
export interface OpenTab {
  id: string
  projectPath: string
  sessionId?: string  // ✓ included
  title: string
  ptyId?: string      // ✓ included (as ptyId)
  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'  // ✓ included
}
```

**However**, in renderer's `OpenTab` (src/renderer/stores/workspace.ts):
```typescript
export interface OpenTab {
  id: string
  projectPath: string
  sessionId?: string
  title: string
  ptyId: string  // Required, not optional!
  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
}
```

**Risk:** Type mismatch could cause undefined ptyId issues during restore.

---

### **5. MEDIUM: Settings Persistence Split Across Multiple Calls**

**Location:** `src/renderer/components/SettingsModal.tsx` lines 158-180

**Issue:** Settings are saved via two separate async calls with no coordination:

```typescript
async function handleSave(): Promise<void> {
  const newSettings = {
    defaultProjectDir: general.defaultProjectDir,
    theme: general.selectedTheme,
    themeCustomization: general.themeCustomization,
    autoAcceptTools: general.autoAcceptTools,
    permissionMode: general.permissionMode,
    backend: general.backend
  }
  await window.electronAPI?.saveSettings(newSettings)  // Call 1
  // NO ERROR HANDLING
  await window.electronAPI?.voiceApplySettings?.({     // Call 2
    ttsVoice: voice.selectedVoice,
    ttsEngine: voice.selectedEngine,
    ttsSpeed: voice.ttsSpeed,
    xttsTemperature: xtts.temperature,
    xttsTopK: xtts.topK,
    xttsTopP: xtts.topP,
    xttsRepetitionPenalty: xtts.repetitionPenalty
  })
  onSaved?.(newSettings)  // !! Reports success before second call completes
  onClose()
}
```

**Problems:**
- If `voiceApplySettings` fails, `onSaved` callback already fired
- Settings are reported saved but voice settings might have failed
- No error boundaries or validation
- No user feedback if either call fails

**Risk:** Partial settings applied - theme saves but voice settings fail silently.

---

### **6. MEDIUM: Empty Workspace Protection Has Edge Case**

**Location:** `src/main/session-store.ts` lines 214-225

**Issue:** The protection against overwriting populated workspaces has a loophole:

```typescript
saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
  const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
  const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
  if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
    console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
    return  // !! Silently returns - no error thrown
  }
  this.data.workspace = workspace
  this.save()
  syncMetaProjects(workspace)
}
```

**Problems:**
- **Silent failure:** Callers don't know the save was rejected
- **Inconsistent state:** `this.data.workspace` was NOT updated, but caller thinks it was
- **No error callback:** No way to notify user or app of rejection
- Renderer component at line 185 calls `api.saveWorkspace()` but has no error handling

**Risk:**
- Frontend state and disk state diverge silently
- User makes changes, thinks they're saved, but they're not
- Next app restart shows old data, losing in-memory changes

---

### **7. MEDIUM: Race Condition in Session Restore and Tab Recreation**

**Location:** `src/renderer/hooks/useWorkspaceLoader.ts` lines 88-156

**Issue:** Complex async tab restoration with potential race conditions:

```typescript
if (workspace.openTabs && workspace.openTabs.length > 0) {
  const currentTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
  const usedSessionIds = new Set(currentTabs.map(t => t.sessionId).filter(Boolean))
  const sessionsCache = new Map<string, { list: { sessionId: string; slug: string }[]; nextIndex: number }>()

  for (const savedTab of workspace.openTabs) {

}
```

- Each `await api.spawnPty()` is a checkpoint where layout can change
- `idMapping` is built during restore but used later - if restore order matters, this breaks

**Risk:** Tabs restored out of order, layout mismatches, lost tabs if spawnPty fails mid-loop.

    54→  viewMode?: 'tabs' | 'tiled'
    55→  tileLayout?: TileLayout[]

    73→    green?: string
